description_long: This object is a classic urban traffic light with a robust, rectangular housing and a decorative cap on top. It features three large, circular signal lights vertically aligned within deep, protective hoods that shield the lenses from sunlight and weather. The housing is predominantly blue, with the signal lights showing the standard red, yellow, and green colors, though the lights themselves are not illuminated in all views. The structure appears to be made primarily of metal, likely steel or aluminum, with glass or polycarbonate lenses for the signal lights. The design is utilitarian yet slightly ornate, suggesting it could be found in a city with historical or decorative street fixtures. The back and sides are solid, with no visible access panels or wiring, and the mounting hardware is not visible, indicating it may be designed for pole or overhead mounting.
description: A blue, vertically-stacked traffic light with three large, circular signal lenses and deep protective hoods, topped with a decorative cap.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three signal lights and their hoods clearly, with the decorative cap centered above.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view revealing the left side of the housing and the depth of the hoods, with the red light most visible.
description_view_2: Rear view with the signal lights and hoods visible but less detail on the lenses, emphasizing the solid back structure.
description_view_3: Slightly rotated view revealing the right side of the housing and the depth of the hoods, with the yellow light most visible.
